title: Contexts
compatibility: kind supplies one isolated kubeconfig context; the multiple-cluster input and restricted client certificate are represented as a fixture.
prompt: Write all kubeconfig context names, one per line, to artifacts/contexts. Decode the client certificate for user restricted@infra-prod into artifacts/cert.

title: Image Vulnerability Scanning
compatibility: requires Trivy and a vulnerability database; stock kind does not provide either.
prompt: Scan the four brief images and write only images without CVE-2020-10878 or CVE-2020-1967 to artifacts/good-images.

title: Apiserver Security
compatibility: a ClusterIP Service can be practised, but stock kind does not expose the kube-apiserver as the brief's NodePort static-pod setup.
prompt: Change the apiserver exposure from NodePort to ClusterIP. Record the resulting Service and apiserver argument evidence in artifacts/apiserver-service.yaml.

title: ServiceAccount Token Expiration
compatibility: ordinary Deployment, ServiceAccount, and projected token resources run on stock kind.
prompt: Update artifacts/stream-multiplex.yaml so Pods have token-lifetime=1200, use ServiceAccount stream-multiplex, disable automount, and mount a 1200-second projected token at /var/run/secrets/custom/; create the Deployment.

title: CIS Benchmark
compatibility: requires kube-bench and host ownership, permissions, and kubelet configuration on separate nodes.
prompt: Use CIS recommendations to set kube-controller-manager --profiling=false, /var/lib/etcd ownership, kubelet config permissions, and kubelet --client-ca-file. Record evidence in artifacts/cis-evidence.txt.

title: Immutable Root FileSystem
compatibility: Pod securityContext and an emptyDir /tmp volume are supported by stock kind.
prompt: Update immutable-deployment in team-purple so its root filesystem is read-only and only /tmp is writable. Save the resulting manifest as artifacts/immutable-deployment-new.yaml and apply it.

title: Pod Security Standard and Admission
compatibility: Pod Security Admission namespace labels and a deliberately non-compliant Pod are supported by Kubernetes 1.35.
prompt: Label team-sepia for baseline audit and restricted warn, create the supplied bad Pod, and capture warnings/errors in artifacts/bad-pod.log.

title: Docker Configuration and Usage
compatibility: kind nodes use containerd and cannot change the host Docker daemon's icc setting from inside the cluster.
prompt: Set Docker daemon icc=false, restart/reload Docker, and create detached always-restarting nginx:1-alpine containers container1 and container2. Record evidence in artifacts/docker-evidence.txt.

title: AppArmor Profile
compatibility: requires an AppArmor-enabled Linux worker and profile installation; Docker Desktop kind nodes do not expose this workflow.
prompt: Install the supplied AppArmor profile, label a worker security=apparmor, deploy nginx with the profile on container c1, and write Pod logs to artifacts/logs.

title: Container Runtime Sandbox gVisor
compatibility: requires runsc/gVisor configured in a worker container runtime; stock kind uses containerd without runsc.
prompt: Create RuntimeClass gvisor with handler runsc and a team-purple Pod gvisor-test pinned to cks7262-node1; record its dmesg output in artifacts/gvisor-test-dmesg.

title: Secret Management
compatibility: Secrets, ConfigMaps, namespace migration, and Deployment references are supported by stock kind.
prompt: Update db-con password to 4c!29f_Ee2e, move user-data to team-khaki-us-east-ad2, convert app-data ConfigMap to a Secret, delete the ConfigMap, and preserve consuming Pods. Record the result in artifacts/secret-migration.yaml.

title: ImagePolicyWebhook
compatibility: requires editing the kube-apiserver static Pod and a webhook backend; stock kind's control plane is not an exam node filesystem.
prompt: Create the ImagePolicyWebhook AdmissionConfiguration, mount the webhook directory, point the apiserver at it, enable the plugin, and prove danger-danger images are denied. Record evidence in artifacts/webhook-evidence.yaml.

title: CiliumNetworkPolicy Metadata Server
compatibility: requires Cilium as the cluster CNI and a reachable metadata endpoint; stock kind installs a non-Cilium CNI.
prompt: In metadata-access create CiliumNetworkPolicy default allowing the required cluster/external egress while denying 192.168.100.21:9055. Save it as artifacts/cilium-policy.yaml.

title: ETCD Secret Encryption
compatibility: requires host-mounted encryption configuration and static kube-apiserver flags; kind does not expose the brief's host paths as an exam node.
prompt: Extract the aesgcm password to artifacts/password.txt, mount /etc/kubernetes/etcd into the apiserver, use ec.yaml, and ensure team-magenta Secrets are encrypted. Record flags in artifacts/encryption-evidence.yaml.

title: Configure TLS on Ingress
compatibility: Ingress and TLS Secret objects are supported, but stock kind has no ingress controller or the brief's host ports by default.
prompt: In team-pink update Ingress secure to use the provided tls.key and tls.crt as a TLS Secret while retaining /app and /api. Save evidence in artifacts/secure-ingress.yaml.

title: Runtime Security with Falco
compatibility: requires Falco plus kernel event capture and host configuration; this is outside a stock kind cluster.
prompt: Add only Custom Rule 1 (WARNING, /etc/kubernetes file access) and Custom Rule 2 (INFO, kill syscalls) to falco_rules.local.yaml, run Falco for at least 30 seconds, and write logs to artifacts/logs.

title: Audit Log Policy
compatibility: requires kube-apiserver audit log host paths and flags on a kubeadm-style control plane; stock kind does not expose them as an exam filesystem.
prompt: Keep one audit-log backup, retain only Secret Metadata events and system:nodes RequestResponse events, and clear the existing log. Save policy and flag evidence in artifacts/audit-policy.yaml.
